key.name: trigger-accels
key.description: List of accelerator strings for the push-to-talk trigger. Any of the listed keys will activate recording. Default is the Pause key. Examples: 'Pause', 'Scroll_Lock', 'F13', '<Ctrl><Shift>d'. Modifier-only keys (Control_R, etc.) are NOT supported.

key.name: release-gap-ms
key.description: Time in milliseconds with no key-repeat events before the key is considered released. Must be longer than your keyboard's repeat delay (typically 250-600ms). Default: 700.

key.name: inter-tap-gap-ms
key.description: Minimum gap in milliseconds between key-repeat events to detect a release-and-repress (inter-tap). Key repeats fire every ~30ms, so gaps larger than this indicate the key was released and pressed again. Default: 60.

key.name: repeat-delay-ms
key.description: Upper bound in milliseconds for inter-tap gap detection. Gaps longer than this are the keyboard repeat delay (key still held), not an inter-tap. Must be shorter than release-gap-ms. Default: 400.

key.name: double-tap-window-ms
key.description: After a quick tap, wait this many milliseconds for a second tap before discarding the recording. Default: 500.

key.name: hold-threshold
key.description: Minimum number of key-repeat events to consider a keypress a "hold" (hold-to-talk) rather than a "tap" (double-tap-to-lock). Quick taps produce 1-3 events; holds produce many more. Default: 5.

key.name: stt-backend
key.description: Which speech recognition engine to use. 'vosk' — gst-vosk (streaming, CPU-based, works now). 'whisper' — GStreamer whisper element (requires GStreamer 1.28.1+).

key.name: audio-input-device
key.description: PulseAudio/PipeWire source device name for recording (e.g. 'alsa_input.pci-0000_04_00.5-platform-nau8821-max.HiFi__Internal_Mic__source'). Leave empty to use the system default input device.

key.name: vosk-model-path
key.description: Path to the VOSK speech recognition model directory. Leave empty to auto-detect from ~/.cache/vosk.

key.name: whisper-model-path
key.description: Path to the Whisper model file (e.g. ggml-base.en.bin). Leave empty to auto-detect from ~/.cache/whisper.

key.name: whisper-language
key.description: Language code for the Whisper backend (e.g. 'en', 'de', 'fr'). Leave empty for auto-detection.

key.name: ai-enabled
key.description: When enabled, raw STT output is sent through an AI model for cleanup before being typed.

key.name: ai-backend
key.description: Which AI backend to use for text cleanup. 'anthropic' — Anthropic Claude API (cloud, requires API key). 'ollama' — Local Ollama server (no API key, runs on your machine).

key.name: anthropic-api-key
key.description: API key for the Anthropic Claude API.

key.name: proxy-url
key.description: HTTP proxy URL for Anthropic API requests (e.g. http://localhost:8080). Leave empty to connect directly.

key.name: proxy-ca-cert
key.description: Path to a PEM file containing the CA certificate for the proxy. Required when using a TLS-intercepting proxy. Leave empty when not using a proxy.

key.name: anthropic-model
key.description: Model ID for the Anthropic API.

key.name: ollama-url
key.description: Base URL for the Ollama server. Default: http://localhost:11434

key.name: ollama-model
key.description: Model name for Ollama (e.g. 'qwen2.5:3b', 'llama3.2:3b', 'qwen2.5:7b'). Must be pulled first with 'ollama pull'.

key.name: system-prompt-path
key.description: Path to a text file containing the AI system prompt. The file is re-read at the start of each recording session, so changes take effect without restarting GNOME Shell. Leave empty to use the built-in default.

key.name: framing-prompt-path
key.description: Path to a text file containing the framing message sent at the start of each recording session. Use {{UUID}} as a placeholder for the per-session UUID. Re-read each session. Leave empty to use the built-in default.

key.name: max-transcripts
key.description: Maximum number of transcripts to keep in history.

key.name: developer-mode
key.description: When enabled, audio files are kept after successful transcription and transcripts (raw STT + cleaned AI output) are saved to disk for QA and debugging purposes.

key.name: transcript-dir
key.description: Directory where dev-mode transcripts and audio files are saved. Leave empty to use ~/.local/share/speakeasy/transcripts.
